developerDoc.details: Provides information about the current execution context, including the sender of the transaction and its data. While these are generally available via msg.sender and msg.data, they should not be accessed in such a direct manner, since when dealing with meta-transactions the account sending and paying for execution may not be the actual sender (as far as an application is concerned). This contract is only required for intermediate, library-like contracts.

developerDoc.details: Contract module which provides a basic access control mechanism, where there is an account (an owner) that can be granted exclusive access to specific functions. The initial owner is set to the address provided by the deployer. This can later be changed with {transferOwnership}. This module is used through inheritance. It will make available the modifier `onlyOwner`, which can be applied to your functions to restrict their use to the owner.

OwnableInvalidOwner(address).details: The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)

OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address).details: The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation.

constructor.details: Initializes the contract setting the address provided by the deployer as the initial owner.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

developerDoc.details: Wrappers over Solidity's uintXX/intXX casting operators with added overflow checks. Downcasting from uint256/int256 in Solidity does not revert on overflow. This can easily result in undesired exploitation or bugs, since developers usually assume that overflows raise errors. `SafeCast` restores this intuition by reverting the transaction when such an operation overflows. Using this library instead of the unchecked operations eliminates an entire class of bugs, so it's recommended to use it always.

SafeCastOverflowedIntDowncast(uint8,int2.details: Value doesn't fit in an int of `bits` size.

SafeCastOverflowedIntToUint(int256).details: An int value doesn't fit in an uint of `bits` size.

SafeCastOverflowedUintDowncast(uint8,uin.details: Value doesn't fit in an uint of `bits` size.

SafeCastOverflowedUintToInt(uint256).details: An uint value doesn't fit in an int of `bits` size.

CalldataPriceSet(uint64,uint256).notice: Emitted when the calldata price is set.

GasPriceSet(uint64,uint256).notice: Emitted when the gas price is set.

NativePriceSet(uint64,uint256).notice: Emitted when the native price is set.

convertRemoteValueToLocalUnits(uint64,ui.notice: Convert a value from the native token of a remote chain to the local native token.

estimateTxCostInLocalUnits(uint64,uint25.notice: Estimate the cost of execution a transaction on a remote chain, and convert it to the local native token.

estimateTxCostInRemoteUnits(uint64,uint2.notice: Estimate the cost of execution a transaction on a remote chain, and return it as is in the remote chain's native token.

getLocalGasData().notice: Gets the gas data for the local chain.

getLocalNativePrice().notice: Gets the price of the local chain's native token in Ethereum Mainnet's wei.

getRemoteGasData(uint64).notice: Gets the gas data for a remote chain.

receiveRemoteGasData(uint64,bytes).notice: Allows Synapse Module to pass the gas data from a remote chain to the Gas Oracle.

setLocalNativePrice(uint256).notice: Allows the contract owner to set the native token price of the local chain.

setRemoteCallDataPrice(uint64,uint256).notice: Allows the contract owner to set the price of remote chain's calldata.

setRemoteGasData(uint64,(uint256,uint256.notice: Allows the contract owner to set the gas data for a remote chain.

setRemoteGasPrice(uint64,uint256).notice: Allows the contract owner to set the gas price of the remote chain.

setRemoteNativePrice(uint64,uint256).notice: Allows the contract owner to set the price of the remote chain's native token.

OwnableInvalidOwner(address).details: The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)

OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address).details: The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation.

SafeCastOverflowedUintDowncast(uint8,uin.details: Value doesn't fit in an uint of `bits` size.

params.chainId: The chain ID of the chain.

params.calldataSize: The size of the transaction calldata.
params.gasLimit: The gas limit of the transaction.
params.remoteChainId: The chain id of the remote chain.

estimateTxCostInRemoteUnits(uint64,uint2.details: Will revert if no price is available for the remote chain.

params.gasLimit: The gas limit of the transaction.
params.remoteChainId: The chain id of the remote chain.

params.chainId: The chain id of the remote chain.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

receiveRemoteGasData(uint64,bytes).details: Could only be called by Synapse Module.

params.data: The gas data from the remote chain.
params.srcChainId: The chain id of the remote chain.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setLocalNativePrice(uint256).details: Could only be called by the contract owner. Will revert if the native token price is 0.

params.nativePrice: The price of the local chain's native token in Ethereum Mainnet's wei.

setRemoteCallDataPrice(uint64,uint256).details: Could only be called by the contract owner. Will revert if the chain id is not a remote chain id, or if native token price for the chain is 0.

params.calldataPrice: The price of 1 byte of calldata in the remote chain's wei.
params.chainId: The chain id of the remote chain.

setRemoteGasData(uint64,(uint256,uint256.details: Could only be called by the contract owner. Will revert if the native token price is 0, or if the chain id is not a remote chain id.

params.chainId: The chain id of the remote chain.
params.data: The gas data for the remote chain.

setRemoteGasPrice(uint64,uint256).details: Could only be called by the contract owner. Will revert if the chain id is not a remote chain id, or if native token price for the chain is 0.

params.chainId: The chain id of the remote chain.
params.gasPrice: The gas price of the remote chain, in remote chain's wei.

setRemoteNativePrice(uint64,uint256).details: Could only be called by the contract owner. Will revert if the chain id is not a remote chain id, or if the price is 0.

params.chainId: The chain id of the remote chain.
params.nativePrice: The price of the remote chain's native token in Ethereum Mainnet's wei.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

_localNativePrice.details: Price of the local chain's native token, expressed in Ethereum Mainnet's wei.

_remoteGasData.details: Gas data for tracked remote chains: - calldataPrice     The price of 1 byte of calldata in the remote chain's wei. - gasPrice          The gas price of the remote chain, in remote chain's wei. - nativePrice       The price of the remote chain's native token in Ethereum Mainnet's wei.
